title: Perfect Your Numbers: Boost UI Readability with CSS font-feature-settings for Tabular Figures
tweet: Elevate your UI design! Ensure perfect alignment and readability for numbers in tables & data displays using CSS 'font-feature-settings'. A small detail, huge UX impact. #CSSTypography #UIDesign #WebFonts #UserExperience
tip: Many web fonts include multiple styles for numbers, such as 'proportional' (natural width) and 'tabular' (fixed width). For data tables, price lists, or any columnar numerical data, using 'tabular' figures is crucial for consistent vertical alignment and enhanced readability. Without it, numbers like '1' and '8' can cause columns to wobble, making comparisons difficult and frustrating for users. Leverage CSS 'font-feature-settings' to activate these stylistic sets, providing a professional and highly legible numeric experience, especially for financial data or statistical reports. This small CSS tweak significantly improves data-heavy UI's user experience.
